summary: No-ship: the slice still misses the promised shy auto-infer path, can emit/stick an invalid mode from config drift, and the prompt/header contract for `[mode:]` delegation is inconsistent.

findings.title: `resolveMode` never produces the promised shy auto-inferred mode path
findings.recommendation: Implement the first-turn shy inference branch (and its `auto_inferred` trigger / switch-log entry) or explicitly remove that requirement from the slice acceptance. As written, the resolver only supports `explicit_name`, `sticky`, and `channel_default`, so unnamed non-default first turns can never be inferred to another face.

findings.title: An invalid configured `defaultMode` resolves to a non-existent soul and then becomes sticky
findings.recommendation: Validate the fallback against `names` before returning it, e.g. fall back to `names[0]` when `defaultMode` is absent or unknown. Also sanitize `modes.defaultMode` when assembling the config so a stale `modes.json` value cannot poison session state.

findings.title: The delegation prompt describes a standalone `[mode: <name>]` header, but the gateway never emits that format
findings.recommendation: Make the emitted turn header match the documented contract exactly, or rewrite the prompt/tests to match the actual emitted header. Today the prompt says to honor `[mode: <name>]`, while `buildOrchestratorTurn` only embeds `mode: <name>` inside `[origin: ..., channel: ...]`, so the core delegation signal is inconsistent and untested end-to-end.
